summary: Verified Google Calendar OAuth login URL generation. /api/auth/google/login now returns a valid Google authorization_url with new client_id (no more 400 'Google credentials not configured').

minor.issue: redirect_uri falls back to http://localhost:8001/api/auth/google/callback because backend/.env does not define REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. OAuth callback from Google will fail in production unless localhost is allowed in Google Console (it cannot be for a deployed URL). The redirect URI registered in Google Cloud Console must match what is sent. Recommendation: set REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://studio-minimal-15.preview.emergentagent.com in backend/.env (or expose a dedicated PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL var) and register that callback URL in Google Cloud Console.

action_items: Set REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in backend/.env to the public preview URL so OAuth redirect_uri is correct (currently http://localhost:8001/...).
Ensure the chosen redirect URI is registered as an Authorized redirect URI in the Google Cloud Console OAuth client.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 292: REDIRECT_URI uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var which is not set in backend/.env – silently falls back to localhost and will break OAuth in deployed env.
Frontend env var name (REACT_APP_*) is being read on the backend side – consider a clearer name like PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL or BACKEND_PUBLIC_URL.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Google OAuth URL generation verified working. Outstanding concern: REDIRECT_URI uses localhost fallback because backend/.env lacks REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. End-to-end Google callback flow has NOT been tested (requires real Google login interaction).
rca of the issue: Previous 400 'Google credentials not configured' resolved – GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET are now correctly loaded from /app/backend/.env after main agent updated them and restarted backend. authorization_url contains the expected new client_id.
